summary: Tested MailMaster PRO anti-blocking layer. 22/28 pytest tests pass. New /api/throttle/defaults endpoint works correctly and returns the expected 7-field response (per_minute=20, per_hour=300, per_day=2000, per_domain_per_min=5, delay_min_ms=800, delay_max_ms=2500, today_sent=0) — the daily_quota table is verified to exist (otherwise current_daily_count() would raise). Storage encryption regression is intact (SQLCipher header still encrypted, stdlib sqlite3 cannot open). CRITICAL bug found: BOTH /api/campaigns/send and /api/external/send return HTTP 500 instead of HTTP 200 when SMTP is unreachable. Per the review request, these endpoints MUST return 200 with campaign_id + sent=0 + failed=N + 'throttle' echo even when SMTP cannot connect — currently they crash before the per-recipient loop runs. This is the same root cause for all 6 failing tests (3 pre-existing regression tests + 3 new throttle tests).

critical.issue: HTTP 500 when SMTP unreachable. SMTPSession.__enter__() (i.e. _connect → socket.gethostbyname for smtp.example.com → gaierror) is called at server.py:351 (send_campaign) and server.py:476 (external_send) OUTSIDE any try/except. The connect failure raises and the per-recipient try/except never executes, so no send_logs are written, no campaign_id is returned, and no 'throttle' echo is returned. Per review request: 'All sends will fail at the SMTPSession.connect() step — that is expected. The campaign endpoints should still return 200 with campaign_id, total/sent=0/failed=N, and write log entries.'

minor.issue: Daily quota wait computes 'next midnight' as 23:59:59 today instead of next-day 00:00 — off by up to 1s, low impact.

record_send is called only on success (per intent), but failed sends still consume SMTP connection attempts and don't update sliding windows — minute/hour caps don't apply to failures. Documented behaviour, but flag for future ESP rate-limit alignment.
_validate_api_key still iterates ALL non-revoked keys and bcrypts each — O(N) per request. Carried over from previous iteration.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): wrap smtp.__enter__() in try/except in /api/campaigns/send and /api/external/send. On connect failure, the endpoint must still iterate recipients and write 'failed' send_logs for each, return 200 with campaign_id, sent=0, failed=N, and the 'throttle' echo. Easiest patch: move `await asyncio.to_thread(smtp.__enter__)` INTO the per-recipient try/except (or set smtp=None and let smtp.send raise inside the existing try). Affected lines: server.py:350-351 and server.py:475-476.
After fix, re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — all 6 failing tests should pass with no further changes.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: SMTPSession context-manager entry is fragile — __enter__ does network I/O that can fail synchronously, but the surrounding code treats it as infallible. Per-recipient try/except must envelope connection as well as send.
throttle.py: ThrottleConfig.as_dict() correctly echoes per-campaign overrides — endpoint contract verified via /api/throttle/defaults.
throttle.py: daily_quota INSERT … ON CONFLICT(day) DO UPDATE is correct SQLite UPSERT syntax (requires SQLite 3.24+, sqlcipher3 ships ≥3.42, OK).
database.py: daily_quota table is created in init_schema (verified — /api/throttle/defaults reads today_sent=0 without error).

email_service.py: SMTPSession.send retry/backoff logic is sound for transient SMTP codes; reconnect on disconnect is correct. Headers (Message-ID, Date, X-Mailer, Reply-To, List-Unsubscribe) are properly added.
server.py: 'throttle' echo correctly returned in successful path — but never reached when SMTP fails to connect upfront.
Backend file size: server.py now 581 lines — getting large. Consider splitting into routers (auth/users/templates/campaigns/throttle/api_keys) for maintainability.
seed_data_creation: Tests create and clean up TEST_-prefixed users, email templates, word templates, and API keys. SMTP-fail send_logs accumulate (intended). daily_quota table populated on startup (currently row count=0 since no successful sends).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the SMTP-connect-failure handling in send_campaign/external_send, re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. All 28 tests should pass. The throttle override tests (TestThrottle::test_campaign_send_echoes_throttle_and_runs_fast, test_external_send_echoes_throttle, test_today_sent_unaffected_by_failed_sends) explicitly verify: (1) 'throttle' echo in response, (2) total/sent=0/failed=N for unreachable SMTP, (3) elapsed time <20s with overrides delay_min/max_ms=0+per_minute=1000, (4) today_sent stays 0 because record_send is success-only. /api/throttle/defaults already verified working (returns all 7 fields + correct types).
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: In server.py the SMTP connection is opened BEFORE the per-recipient loop, on lines 350-351 (send_campaign) and 475-476 (external_send): `smtp = await asyncio.to_thread(SMTPSession); await asyncio.to_thread(smtp.__enter__)`. The __enter__ call invokes _connect() which performs DNS resolution on SMTP_HOST=smtp.example.com → socket.gaierror[Errno -2]. This exception propagates out of the route handler → FastAPI returns 500. REPRO: POST /api/campaigns/send with any valid form payload → 500 Internal Server Error. MITIGATION: Move smtp.__enter__() inside a try/except that, on failure, marks ALL recipients as failed and writes per-recipient send_logs with the connect error. Alternatively, lazy-connect inside smtp.send (catch in existing per-recipient try). Minimal patch (5 lines): wrap line 351 (and 476) with try/except, set smtp=None on failure, and inside the loop check `if smtp is None: raise last_connect_err` so the existing per-recipient except writes the failed log.
